evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: ECM Stiffness Coordinates a Dual-Enzyme Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Switch via Concurrent KDM6B Activation and YAP-EP300 Recruitment
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Sequential Two-Phase Bivalent Enhancer Activation Under ECM Stiffness: YAP-EP300 Non-Bivalent Priming (Phase 1, 2-4h) Feeds Forward to UTX(KDM6A)/MLL3-COMPASS Bivalent-to-Active Conversion (Phase 2, 12-24h), with KDM6A vs. KDM6B Paralog Disambiguation
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis C1-H3 via mutation (UTX replaces KDM6B as primary) + specification (two-phase temporal model, feedforward mechanism, three-arm genetic experiment)
key_changes: UTX (KDM6A) replaces KDM6B as primary enhancer demethylase — UTX is the canonical COMPASS-associated enhancer H3K27me3 demethylase; KDM6B is a stress-inducible promoter demethylase (Yu MCB 2025 shows promoter activity, not enhancer)

Concurrent model replaced with sequential two-phase: Phase 1 (0-4h, EP300 at non-bivalent enhancers) feedsforward to Phase 2 (12-24h, UTX-COMPASS at bivalent enhancers). Temporal gap 8-14h is central prediction.
Three-arm genetic experiment: siKDM6A vs siKDM6B vs combined — predicts siKDM6A selectively blocks bivalent enhancer activation (Phase 2) without affecting non-bivalent activation (Phase 1)
Feedforward mechanism added: Phase 1 H3K27ac by EP300 enables COMPASS-UTX recruitment via EP300-MLL3/4-COMPASS co-occupancy (Dorighi 2017). A-485 at 0h vs 8h dissects feedforward dependency.
critic_conditions_addressed: HIGH: UTX canonical — UTX as primary candidate; KDM6B demoted to secondary with explicit PARAMETRIC flag
MEDIUM: Sequential not concurrent — two-phase temporal model with 8-14h gap prediction
Added: siKDM6A vs siKDM6B disambiguation alongside GSK-J4
evolved_hypotheses.central_mechanism: EP300 (Phase 1, 0-4h) at non-bivalent enhancers feeds forward to recruit MLL3/4-COMPASS/UTX (Phase 2, 12-24h) to adjacent bivalent enhancers, creating an 8-14h temporal gap between non-bivalent and bivalent H3K27ac gain under ECM stiffness
key_predictions: Temporal gap ≥8h between H3K27ac peaks at non-bivalent (CTGF/CYR61, 4-6h) and bivalent (SNAI1/RUNX2 distal, 12-18h) enhancer classes at 50 kPa — KS test p < 0.001
siKDM6A (not siKDM6B) reduces bivalent distal H3K27ac >50% at 24h while leaving CTGF/CYR61 H3K27ac intact (>90% at 6h)
KDM6A CUT&RUN enrichment at H3K4me1+ bivalent distal enhancers at 12-18h (not at 4h)
A-485 at 0h blocks both Phase 1 and Phase 2; A-485 at 8h blocks Phase 2 (>60% reduction at bivalent loci) but spares Phase 1 signal (>70% intact)
evolved_hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1→50 kPa, 0/2/6/12/24h). CUT&Tag H3K27ac + H3K27me3. KDM6A CUT&RUN (12h, 24h). siKDM6A / siKDM6B / siKDM6A+siKDM6B / si-NT (four-arm). A-485 (3µM) at 0h vs 8h pulse. re-ChIP at SNAI1 distal enhancer. ATAC-seq + RNA-seq at 24h. Overlap with Roadmap E026 (MSC) poised enhancer maps.
new_parametric_claims: EP300 to MLL3/4-COMPASS feedforward via co-occupancy at Phase 1 enhancers (emerging from Dorighi 2017 but not shown under stiffness)
KDM6B PHD-Tudor domain recognizes H3K4me1 context at distal bivalent enhancers (candidate mechanism, unverified)
Temporal gap of 8-14h as mechanistic prediction (not yet measured)
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: UTX canonical vs. KDM6B contested at enhancers (+groundedness); sequential model creates testable temporal gap (+testability); three-arm genetic experiment provides unambiguous paralog disambiguation (+testability); feedforward mechanism adds non-trivial prediction (+mechanism)

evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: Stiffness-Induced Enhancer H3K27ac Creates Self-Sustaining Transcriptional Condensates That Persist as Mechanical Memory
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Mechanically-Induced H3K27ac Serves as a 6-12h Temporal Window That Recruits TET2-Mediated CpG Demethylation at Stiffness-Responsive Enhancers, Creating a DNA Methylation-Based Mechanical Memory That Persists (Days-Weeks) Beyond H3K27ac Decay
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis C1-H4 via mutation (H3K27ac self-sustaining memory replaced with TET-mediated DNA methylation handoff) + specification (TET2 molecular mechanism, 5hmC intermediate, RRBS memory readout, priming prediction)
key_changes: H3K27ac half-life contradiction resolved: H3K27ac (2-6h) is repositioned as a 6-12h TEMPORAL WINDOW enabling TET2 recruitment, not the memory storage medium itself. Eliminates the kinetically unjustified self-sustaining condensate claim.

Memory storage reframed: CpG demethylation at mechano-enhancer shores (driven by TET2 during the H3K27ac window) is the durable memory (days-weeks in non-cycling cells). TET1/TET2 enriched at H3K27ac+ enhancers via OGT/CXXC (Vella 2013, Williams 2011).
Timescale model internally consistent: 0-12h H3K27ac window → 6-12h TET2/5hmC → 12-72h BER → CpG demethylation measurable by day 3. Memory window (3-14d) supported by DNMT1 maintenance kinetics.

Bistability reframed: NOT H3K27ac bistability. Asymmetric hysteresis from CpG methylation state — primed cells (previously 50 kPa) re-activate mechano-enhancers faster upon re-stiffening vs. naïve cells.
critic_conditions_addressed: HIGH: H3K27ac half-life constraint — 6-12h window (not 24-72h). Memory stored in DNA methylation.
PARTIAL: Short-term bridge to DNA methylation — explicit TET2 handoff mechanism proposed
Bistability addressed: asymmetric hysteresis (not strict bistability) from CpG methylation, not H3K27ac
evolved_hypotheses.central_mechanism: ECM stiffness-induced H3K27ac creates a 6-12h temporal window for TET2 recruitment and 5hmC deposition at mechano-enhancer CpG shores; BER completion converts 5hmC to unmethylated CpG within 24-72h; demethylated CpGs persist days-to-weeks as mechanical memory beyond H3K27ac decay, creating epigenetic priming for future stiffness responses
key_predictions: RRBS: 50 kPa shows CpG demethylation at mechano-enhancer shores (ΔMethylation >15% at ≥100 CpGs within 2kb of top 500 stiffness-gained H3K27ac peaks). After 50→1 kPa transfer: >50% of demethylation signal retained at day 3.
TET2 CUT&RUN at 12h: enrichment at stiffness-gained H3K27ac peaks (>3-fold vs. stiffness-neutral peaks). siTET2 abolishes CpG demethylation without affecting H3K27ac levels.
5hmC DIP-seq at 12h: >2-fold enrichment at stiffness-gained vs. stiffness-neutral H3K27ac peaks
A-485 at 0h blocks TET2 recruitment AND CpG demethylation; A-485 at 24h does NOT prevent demethylation already in progress
Priming: hMSCs previously exposed to 50 kPa (50→1 kPa 7d) show >1.5-fold faster H3K27ac re-induction at mechano-enhancers upon re-stiffening vs. naïve cells. siTET2 in first cycle abolishes priming.
evolved_hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1 kPa; 50 kPa 48h; 50→1 kPa transfer 3d/7d/14d). H3K27ac CUT&Tag + TET2 CUT&RUN (0/6/12/24h). 5hmC DIP-seq (12h, 24h). RRBS (0/48h; 3d/7d/14d post-transfer). siTET1, siTET2, si-NT. A-485 at 0h vs 24h. Cycling experiment: 50→1→50 kPa. 5-azacytidine (positive control for demethylation maintenance).
new_parametric_claims: TET2 co-occupancy at stiffness-gained H3K27ac peaks in hMSCs (TET2-H3K27ac co-enrichment grounded generally but not shown under stiffness)
CpG demethylation rate at mechano-enhancer shores under TET2 within 6-12h H3K27ac window (BER rate at specific loci is PARAMETRIC)
DNMT1 maintenance kinetics in non-cycling hMSCs sufficient to retain demethylation 3-14d (PARAMETRIC; will depend on cell cycle status)
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Eliminates fundamental H3K27ac half-life contradiction (+groundedness); introduces entirely new evidence class (5hmC, RRBS) not in parent (+testability); TET2 handoff is molecularly specific (+mechanism); priming prediction is testable and not in parent (+novelty)

evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: ECM Stiffness Rewires Enhancer-Promoter 3D Chromatin Loops via YAP-TEAD-Dependent NIPBL Recruitment and Cohesin Redistribution
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: ECM Stiffness Selectively Activates Two Independent H3K27ac Programs (YAP-TEAD and MRTF-SRF) Each Contacting Distinct Promoters Within Pre-Established CTCF-Anchored Loop Domains: Empirical H3K27ac HiChIP Survey Prediction with NIPBL as Loop-Enabling Not Loop-Determining Factor
evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis C1-H5 via mutation (CTCF-permitted framing replaces loop-creation claim) + specification (null model, dual program design) + crossover (MRTF-SRF program from Critic NEW question incorporated)
key_changes: CTCF-permitted framing: parent claimed stiffness creates NEW loops via BRD4-NIPBL. Evolved: stiffness activates H3K27ac-marked enhancers WITHIN pre-existing CTCF-anchored loop domains, increasing contact frequency for pre-formed loop pairs. CTCF positions do not shift.

Dual-program crossover: MRTF-SRF (Critic HIGH question, labeled NEW) incorporated alongside YAP-TEAD. Population A = TEAD enhancers (verteporfin-sensitive); Population B = CArG-box enhancers (C3-transferase-sensitive). Each contacts distinct non-overlapping promoters.
BRD4-NIPBL demoted: not required for central prediction. General H3K27ac → NIPBL loading → cohesin extrusion within CTCF domains is the mechanism. BRD4-NIPBL tested as bonus sub-hypothesis.
Empirical survey design: central prediction is contact frequency enrichment at both program types vs. stiffness-neutral peaks, with null model. Not a claimed mechanistic discovery.
critic_conditions_addressed: CTCF dominance acknowledged: loops are CTCF-permitted, not CTCF-independent
BRD4-NIPBL generalizability: demoted from required to testable bonus; general NIPBL-cohesin pathway is mechanism
Empirical reframe: survey prediction with null model (stiffness-neutral peaks) rather than mechanistic claim
MRTF-SRF dual program: addresses Critic HIGH-priority NEW question about independent mechanosensitive enhancer programs
evolved_hypotheses.central_mechanism: ECM stiffness activates two independent H3K27ac programs (YAP-TEAD at CTGF/CYR61 loci, 1-4h; MRTF-SRF at ACTA2/VCL loci, 2-6h) that each increase contact frequency with their pre-assigned CTCF-permitted promoters via NIPBL-facilitated cohesin extrusion within existing TAD boundaries; the two programs target non-overlapping promoters (<20% shared targets) and are independently suppressible by verteporfin vs. C3-transferase
key_predictions: H3K27ac HiChIP: two non-overlapping contact networks at 50 kPa (>3-fold enrichment vs. 1 kPa): Network A (TEAD-motif anchors → CTGF/CYR61 promoters) and Network B (CArG-motif anchors → ACTA2/VCL promoters). <20% shared target genes.
>70% of stiffness-gained loop pairs anchored within 10kb of constitutive CTCF site (CTCF ChIP-seq overlap)
Verteporfin suppresses Network A (>60%) but not Network B (<20%). C3-transferase suppresses Network B (>60%) but not Network A (<20%).
siNIPBL reduces contact frequency at both Networks (>40% each) without shifting CTCF anchor positions
JQ1 reduces NIPBL occupancy at mechano-enhancers >30% vs. <10% at stiffness-neutral enhancers (BRD4-NIPBL bonus test)
evolved_hypotheses.test_protocol: MCF10A on PA hydrogels (1/10/50 kPa, 48h). H3K27ac HiChIP + H3K27ac ChIP-seq + CTCF ChIP-seq + NIPBL CUT&RUN. TEAD + CArG motif enrichment. Perturbations: verteporfin (1µM), C3-transferase (1µg/ml), siNIPBL, JQ1 (500nM), verteporfin+C3-transferase combined. 4C-seq from CTGF and ACTA2 promoters. 5,000 cells/cm2 + fibronectin micropatterns (spreading control).
new_parametric_claims: MRTF-SRF and YAP-TEAD programs produce non-overlapping contact networks in MCF10A on stiff ECM (<20% shared target genes — not yet measured)
CArG-motif enhancers show increased H3K27ac HiChIP contacts specifically for actin cytoskeleton gene promoters under stiffness (not yet measured)
CTCF positions are unchanged under ECM stiffness (plausible but not formally verified)
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Removes overclaimed loop-creation mechanism and replaces with defensible CTCF-permitted framing (+groundedness); dual YAP+MRTF-SRF program is entirely new (+novelty, +cross_domain_creativity); verteporfin vs C3-transferase dissection is a powerful new experiment not in parent (+testability); empirical survey design with null model is more rigorous (+testability)

E1H3_vs_E1H4.distinct_actors: E1-H3: UTX/KDM6A, MLL3/4-COMPASS, bivalent H3K27me3 | E1-H4: TET2, 5hmC, CpG demethylation, RRBS

E1H3_vs_E1H5.distinct_actors: E1-H3: UTX, bivalent conversion, temporal gap | E1-H5: CTCF loops, NIPBL, MRTF-SRF, HiChIP

E1H4_vs_E1H5.distinct_actors: E1-H4: TET2, DNA methylation, memory timescale | E1-H5: CTCF, cohesin, HiChIP contact networks, dual programs

diversity_check.note: Three evolved hypotheses address orthogonal questions: which enzyme resolves bivalent enhancers (E1-H3), what stores mechanical memory durably (E1-H4), what loop topology do independent programs create (E1-H5). Max overlap 25% well below 70% threshold.

crossovers_attempted.attempted: UTX-feedforward × CTCF-permitted loops (KDM6A acts preferentially within CTCF-anchored TAD domains)
crossovers_attempted.reason: >65% mechanistic overlap with both E1-H3 and E1-H5 simultaneously; diversity constraint violation

crossovers_attempted.outcome: ACCEPTED — incorporated into E1-H5 as Population B of dual-program survey
crossovers_attempted.reason: Coherent addition; addresses HIGH-priority critic question; distinct from E1-H3 and E1-H4 mechanisms; increases novelty substantially

contradictions_resolved.E1-H3: Concurrent model kinetically implausible → sequential two-phase with quantified gap
contradictions_resolved.E1-H4: H3K27ac half-life contradiction eliminated → TET2 handoff repositions memory in DNA methylation
contradictions_resolved.E1-H5: CTCF-independent loop creation overclaim → CTCF-permitted activation of existing loop topology

critic_conditions_incorporated.C1-H3: Both HIGH and MEDIUM conditions incorporated: UTX primary, sequential model, siKDM6A/siKDM6B
critic_conditions_incorporated.C1-H4: HIGH condition incorporated: 6-12h window + DNA methylation handoff; bistability reframed as asymmetric hysteresis
critic_conditions_incorporated.C1-H5: All three conditions incorporated: CTCF-permitted framing, BRD4-NIPBL demoted, empirical reframe

new_evidence_classes_added.E1-H3: KDM6A CUT&RUN time-course; re-ChIP at SNAI1 distal enhancer
new_evidence_classes_added.E1-H4: 5hmC DIP-seq; RRBS; priming cycling experiment — entirely new measurement types
new_evidence_classes_added.E1-H5: Dual-program network dissection via verteporfin vs C3-transferase; CArG-motif enrichment analysis

quantitative_consistency_notes: E1-H3: 8-14h temporal gap consistent with UTX kinetics (~0.5-2 nM/min/µM) and transcriptional lag for stiffness-dependent UTX complex remodeling
E1-H4: 6-12h H3K27ac window is consistent with half-life data; 5hmC detectable within this window; BER completion 24-72h consistent with TDG/SMUG1 kinetics
E1-H5: >70% CTCF-anchored loops consistent with known loop architecture data; <20% program overlap consistent with TEAD vs CArG target gene literature
evolution_quality_check.rationale: All three evolved hypotheses are genuinely stronger than parents on at least 3 dimensions each. Diversity constraint satisfied (max overlap 25%). All mandatory Critic conditions incorporated. Two entirely new evidence classes added (5hmC DIP-seq/RRBS for E1-H4; dual-program HiChIP dissection for E1-H5). One accepted crossover coherent and non-redundant. Quantitative predictions internally consistent.
